put.summary: Purchase Orders 10.0 - Update the requested resource
put.description:
### Purchase Orders 10.0 - Update the requested resource

Update the one resource with the passed in identifier

Source OpenAPI file: ethosapis/BannerEedmAPIs/purchase-orders-10.0.0/purchase-orders.yaml

---

### Purchase Orders 11.0 - Update the requested resource from FPBPOHD and FGBENCH

Travel type purchase order:
A travel purchase order can be adjusted with an update request and can be closed when header status is sent as 'closed'.

Procurement/eProcurement type purchase order: 
• An update request sent for an inProgress purchase order updates the existing document with the changes. A change order will not be created.

• For a purchase order with inProgress status if an update request is sent with status = outstanding, the API completes the inProgress document if the document header amount matches the totals of line item amounts and sets the document complete indicator to Y (FPBPOHD_COMPLETE_IND = Y).

• If an update request is sent with header status as 'voided' for an inProgress purchase order, the API deletes the inProgress purchase order and returns an empty response.

• An update request sent for an outstanding purchase order creates a change order.

• An update request sent with header status 'voided' for an outstanding purchase order will cancel the purchase order and all the associated change orders. If this purchase order is of eProcurement type, the associated requisition is also cancelled.

• Only the document which is completed, approved and posted can be cancelled with an update request.

In addition to the required properties defined in the data model, the following are required for a PUT request: lineItems.accountDetails.allocation


Source OpenAPI file: ethosapis/BannerEedmAPIs/purchase-orders-11.0.0/purchase-orders.yaml

---

### Purchase Orders 11.1.0 - Update the requested resource from FPBPOHD and FGBENCH

Travel type purchase order:
A travel purchase order can be adjusted with an update request and can be closed when header status or line-item status is sent as 'closed'.

Procurement/eProcurement type purchase order:

• An update request sent for an inProgress purchase order updates the existing document with the changes. A change order will not be created.

• For a purchase order with inProgress status if an update request is sent with status = outstanding, the API completes the inProgress document if the document header amount matches the totals of line item amounts and sets the document complete indicator to Y (FPBPOHD_COMPLETE_IND = Y).

• If an update request is sent with header status as 'voided' for an inProgress purchase order, the API deletes the inProgress purchase order and returns an empty response.

• An update request sent for an outstanding purchase order creates a change order.

• An update request sent with header status 'voided' for an outstanding purchase order will cancel the purchase order and all the associated change orders. If this purchase order is of eProcurement type, the associated requisition is also cancelled.

• Only the document which is completed, approved and posted can be cancelled with an update request.

• An update request sent with line-item level status with status = closed, the purchase order will close the line item. If this purchase order is of eProcurement type, the associated requisition’s line item is also closed.

• An update request sent with document level status = closed, the purchase order will close the whole purchase order. If this purchase order is of eProcurement type, the associated requisition is also closed.

• Only the document which is completed, approved and posted can be closed with an update request.


In addition to the required properties defined in the data model, the following are required for a PUT request: lineItems.accountDetails.allocation


Source OpenAPI file: ethosapis/BannerEedmAPIs/purchase-orders-11.1.0/purchase-orders.yaml

---

### Purchase Orders 11.4.0 - Update the requested resource from FPBPOHD and FGBENCH

Travel type purchase order:
A travel purchase order can be adjusted with an update request and can be closed when header status or line-item status is sent as 'closed'.

Procurement/eProcurement type purchase order:

• An update request sent for an inProgress purchase order updates the existing document with the changes. A change order will not be created.

• For a purchase order with inProgress status if an update request is sent with status = outstanding, the API completes the inProgress document if the document header amount matches the totals of line item amounts and sets the document complete indicator to Y (FPBPOHD_COMPLETE_IND = Y).

• If an update request is sent with header status as 'voided' for an inProgress purchase order, the API deletes the inProgress purchase order and returns an empty response.

• An update request sent for an outstanding purchase order creates a change order.

• An update request sent with header status 'voided' for an outstanding purchase order will cancel the purchase order and all the associated change orders. If this purchase order is of eProcurement type, the associated requisition is also cancelled.

• Only the document which is completed, approved and posted can be cancelled with an update request.

• An update request sent with line-item level status with status = closed, the purchase order will close the line item. If this purchase order is of eProcurement type, the associated requisition’s line item is also closed.

• An update request sent with document level status = closed, the purchase order will close the whole purchase order. If this purchase order is of eProcurement type, the associated requisition is also closed.

• Only the document which is completed, approved and posted can be closed with an update request.


In addition to the required properties defined in the data model, the following are required for a PUT request: lineItems.accountDetails.allocation

put.summary: Requisitions 11.0 - Update the requested resource from FPBREQH
put.description:
### Requisitions 11.0 - Update the requested resource from FPBREQH

A requisition record cannot be updated. If an update request is sent with 'status' as cancelled at header level, the API cancels the purchase requisition.

Note: Only the requisition which is completed, approved and posted can be cancelled with an update request.

The payment records where the category of award fund associated with the payment is mapped to 'restricted' in the API configuration setting 'FINANCIAL.AID.FUNDS.PRIVACY' are retrieved. post.summary: Section Instructors 10.0 - Create a new resource in SIRASGN
post.description:
### Section Instructors 10.0 - Create a new resource in SIRASGN

POST requests must include a nil GUID value ("00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000") for the root id property.

Creates a new section instructor record in SIRASGN with given data in the request body

Below are the API behavior details for instructionalEvents, instructionalMethod, workLoad, workStartOn and workEndOn properties on a create request:

Instructional Events:

- Instructional event sent in the request should be valid for the section sent.
- Instructional event should be sent in the request for a traditional section so that SIRASGN_CATEGORY can be tied to SSRMEET_CATAGORY, else the API rejects the request with an error message.
- For an OLR section, it is not required to define a meeting record therefore instructional event is not expected in the request.

Instructional Method:

- Instructional method sent in the request will not a stored in any Banner tables, but will be validated against the corresponding SSRMEET and SSBSECT records.
- If an instructional event exists for the section in SSRMEET, then the instructional method sent in request should match one of the SSRMEET_SCHD_CODE values - else the API rejects the request with an error message.

- If there are no matching instructional event record for the section in SSRMEET, then the instructional method sent in request should match one of the SSBSECT_SCHD_CODE values - else the API rejects the request with an error message.

Work Load:

- Work load sent in the request will not a stored in any Banner tables, but will be validated against the corresponding SSRMEET record.
- If an instructional event exists for the section in SSRMEET, then the work load value must add up to the sum of SCBCRSE_WORKLOAD values - else the API rejects the request with an error message.
- If there are no matching instructional event record for the section in SSRMEET, then the only allowed value for work load is 0 - else the API rejects the request with an error message.

Work Start On:

- Work start on sent in the request will not a stored in any Banner tables, but will be validated against the corresponding SSRMEET record.
- If an instructional event exists for the section in SSRMEET, then the work start on value must the earliest SSRMEET_START_DATE value - else the API rejects the request with an error message.

Work End On:

- Work end  on sent in the request will not a stored in any Banner tables, but will be validated against the corresponding SSRMEET record.
- If an instructional event exists for the section in SSRMEET, then the work end on value must the latest SSRMEET_START_DATE value - else the API rejects the request with an error message.

### Section Registration Statuses 8.0 - Return all resources from STVRSTS

Returns a paged listing of all the section registration statuses from STVRSTS table.

The 'status' property in the API is retrieved based on the STVRSTS_CODE mapped in API setting REGISTRATION.STATUS.REASON.V4. The voice type value stored in STVRSTS_VOICE_TYPE for the STVRSTS_CODE should match the mappings.
- The API publishes status.registrationStatus as 'registered', if the STVRSTS_CODE has STVRSTS_VOICE_TYPE ='R'
- The API publishes status.registrationStatus as 'notRegistered', if the STVRSTS_CODE has STVRSTS_VOICE_TYPE ='W' or 'D' or 'L'
- If the voice type and mappings in API settings do not match the API returns error.

parameters.name: criteria
parameters.description:
### Titles Value  
The title for the section associated with the type.
```
/sections?criteria={"titles":[{"value":"Astronomy Lab"}]}
```
### Start On  
The starting date at which a section may be scheduled to be taken.
```
/sections?criteria={"startOn": "2015-08-29"}
```
### End On  
The ending date at which a section may no longer be scheduled to be taken.
```
/sections?criteria={"endOn": "2025-08-29"}
```
### Code  
A code that may be used to identify a section
```
/sections?criteria={"code": "10001"}
```
### Number  
A numbering scheme or other mark that distinguishes between multiple sections.
```
/sections?criteria={"number": "1"}
```
### Instructional Platform  
A technology platform used to manage the administration of a section.
```
/sections?criteria={"instructionalPlatform": {"id": "80d49491-00ff-483e-aab5-da76fea6528d"}}
```
### Academic Period  
The academic time period associated with a section.
```
/sections?criteria={"academicPeriod": {"id": "8b5f95a3-677f-4846-a329-3eb54a670111"}}
```
### Course  
The smallest unit of instruction for which an organization grants credits.
```
/sections?criteria={"course": {"id": "39e7cfb1-e045-44d1-8446-7e3d55a33ea0"}}
```
### Academic Levels  
The levels of academic progress that can be associated with a section.
```
/sections?criteria={"academicLevels": [{"id": "c6aab4d0-d3c7-40d9-96a6-09db8deaf403"}]}
```
### Site  
The primary location within the organization where a section's meetings will be held.
```
/sections?criteria={"site": {"id": "0ed94aa2-dc31-494c-99a7-f4c272fa6416"}}
```
### Status Category  
The category of the section status.
```
/sections?criteria={"status":{"category":"open"}}
```
### Owning Institution Units  
The units (departments) of the educational institution which own, or are responsible for, a course
```
/sections?criteria={"owningInstitutionUnits": [{"institutionUnit":{"id":"b23bb183-7d73-4726-af1b-661510324c88"}}]}
```
### Reporting Academic Period  
The reporting academic period associated with the section.
```
/sections?criteria={"reportingAcademicPeriod": {"id": "060d0dd1-75aa-4c43-989c-0015b6f01d6e"}}
```

post.summary: Sections 16.0.0 - Create a new resource in SSBSECT
post.description:
### Sections 16.0.0 - Create a new resource in SSBSECT

POST requests must include a nil GUID value ("00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000") for the root id property.

Creates a new section record in SSBSECT with given data in the request body. A create request sent with academic period GUID that is a part of term will create a traditional section. A create request sent with academic period GUID that is an SOBODTE rule will create an open learning section.

Minimum required properties for a open learning (OLR) section POST request:  id, titles, startOn, course, instructionalMethods, academicPeriod, duration

Minimum required properties for a traditional section POST request: id, titles, startOn, course, instructionalMethods, academicPeriod

Below are the API behavior details for titles, description, startOn, endOn, code, number, censusDates, creditType and owningInstitutionUnits properties on a create request:

Titles:

- The API accepts 4 types of titles - short title and long title of the section, short title and long title of the course associated with the section.
- The section short title sent in the request should not exceed 30 characters, similarly the section long title sent in the request should not exceed •0 characters.
- The course short title and long title sent in the request should match the titles of the course record, else the API rejects the request with an error message.

Description:

- The API supports 3 types of descriptions - short, long and schedule.
- Short description is restricted to 60 characters, if more than 60 characters is sent in the request then the API inserts additional record in SSRTEXT table.
- If schedule type description is sent, the create request is rejected with an error message.

startOn & endOn:

- For a traditional section - If section override indicator SOBPTRM_SECT_OVER_IND is set to 'Y' then the startOn date is stored in SSBSECT_PTRM_START_DATE, else if SOBPTRM_SECT_OVER_IND is 'N' then the create request is rejected with an error message.
- For a traditional section - If section override indicator SOBPTRM_SECT_OVER_IND is set to 'Y' then the endOn date is stored in SSBSECT_PTRM_END_DATE, else if SOBPTRM_SECT_OVER_IND is 'N' then the create request is rejected with an error message.
- For an open learning section, the learner start date in SSBSECT_LEARNER_REGSTART_FDATE and end date in SSBSECT_LEARNER_REGSTART_TDATE can be adjusted regardless of the override setting SOBODTE_OVERRIDE_IND.

Code:

- If the value mapped in API configuration setting 'SECTION.EXTERNAL.CRN.ALLOW' is 'Y', then the POST request allows an external partner to send in a predetermined CRN in the code property. If code is not sent in the request then the API uses Banner baseline one-up function to uniquely generate a CRN.
- When the API configuration setting is set to 'Y' - The CRN sent in the request will be accepted only if the provided value is less than the one-up defined in SOBTERM_CRN_ONEUP and is  unique to the term in SSBSECT table.
- If the value mapped in API configuration setting 'SECTION.EXTERNAL.CRN.ALLOW' is 'N', then the POST request will not allow an external partner to send in a predetermined CRN. The API uses Banner baseline one-up function to uniquely generate a CRN. If code property is sent in the request then the create request is rejected with an error message.

Number:

- If the number value provided is equal to 0, there can be multiple sections in SSBSECT for the same subject, course number, term, sequence number.
- If the number value provided is not equal to 0, there can be only one section in SSBSECT for the same subject, course number, term, sequence number. If another section with the same term, course number and sequence number is found, then the create request is rejected with an error message.

Census Dates:

- Census dates for a traditional section is dependent on the override indicator - SOBPTRM_SECT_OVER_IND. When SOBPTRM_SECT_OVER_IND = 'Y', a create request will be rejected if more than 2 census dates are sent. When SOBPTRM_SECT_OVER_IND = 'N', the request is rejected with an error message that overrides are not permitted.
- Census dates for an open learning section is dependent on the override indicator - SOBODTE_OVERRIDE_IND. When SOBODTE_OVERRIDE_IND = 'Y', a create request will be rejected if more than 2 census dates are sent. When SOBODTE_OVERRIDE_IND = 'N', the request is rejected with an error message that overrides are not permitted.

Credit Type:

- The API supports academic credit category of type 'ce' and 'institution' only.
- If the value sent in the create request does not match the credit type associated with the course record, request is rejected with an error message.

Owning Institution Units:

- Banner can only accommodate one college, one division, and one department entry. If more than one owning institution unit of each type is sent in the owningIntitutionUnits array, the request is requested with an error message.
- The college, division and department values sent in the request is compared with the course record in SCBCRSE effective for the the section term. If the values sent in the request do not match with course value then college, division and department information is stored in SSBOVRR table.


Source OpenAPI file: ethosapis/BannerEedmAPIs/sections-16.0.0/sections.yaml

get.summary: Student Academic Credentials 1.0.0 - Return all resources from SORLCUR
get.description:
### Student Academic Credentials 1.0.0 - Return all resources from SORLCUR

Returns a paged listing of all the outcome curriculum records associated with an awarded degree/outcome record. In addition to publishing the current and active programs from SORLCUR table and associated fields of study from SORLFOS table, graduation and honors information are also retrieved from SHRDGMR table.

Source OpenAPI file: ethosapis/BannerEedmAPIs/student-academic-credentials-1.0.0/student-academic-credentials.yaml

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of resources requesting for this result set. Default page limit is 500 and upper limit is 1000.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: The 0 based index for a collection of resources for the page requested.

parameters.name: criteria
parameters.description:
### Student  
The student who has been awarded an academic credential.
```
/student-academic-credentials?criteria={"student":{"id":"4a0c95f7-0903-408a-8694-77b313809909"}}
```
### Student Program  
The academic program associated with the academic credential.
```
/student-academic-credentials?criteria={"studentProgram":{"id":"3efc95f7-0903-408a-8694-77b313806639"}}
```
### Academic Level  
The academic level associated with the academic credential.
```
/student-academic-credentials?criteria={"academicLevel":{"id":"8tfc95f7-0903-408a-8694-77b313806609"}}
```
### Credentials  
The academic credential that has been awarded.
```
/student-academic-credentials?criteria={"credentials":[{"credential":{"id":"7a6e9b82-e78b-47db-9f30-5becff004921"}}]}
```
### Graduation Academic Period  
The academic period in which the student graduated.
```
/student-academic-credentials?criteria={"graduationAcademicPeriod":{"id":"b24b5455-e030-4035-88a0-ef999e9d4171"}}
```
### Graduated On  
Supported operators are $gt, $gte, $lt, $lte, or the default of equals if operator is not specified.

Returns all student academic credentials for the graduated on date passed.
```
/student-academic-credentials?criteria={"graduatedOn":"1995-02-06"}
```
```
/student-academic-credentials?criteria={"graduatedOn":{"$eq":"1995-01-01"}}
```
Returns all student academic credentials with graduated on date less than the graduated on date passed.
```
/student-academic-credentials?criteria={"graduatedOn":{"$lt":"1995-01-01"}}
```
Returns all student academic credentials with graduated on date less than equals to the graduated on date passed.
```
/student-academic-credentials?criteria={"graduatedOn":{"$lte":"1995-01-01"}}
```
Returns all student academic credentials with graduated on date greater than the graduated on date passed.
```
/student-academic-credentials?criteria={"graduatedOn":{"$gt":"1995-01-01"}}
```
Returns all student academic credentials with graduated on date greater than equals to the graduated on date passed.
```
/student-academic-credentials?criteria={"graduatedOn":{"$gte":"1995-01-01"}}
```


post.summary: Student Academic Programs Replacements 1.0.0 - Create a new resource in SORLCUR
post.description:
### Student Academic Programs Replacements 1.0.0 - Create a new resource in SORLCUR

Creates a LEARNER curriculum record in SORLCUR with SORLCUR_LMOD_CODE='LEARNER' and creates the associated field of study records in SORLFOS for an effective term by replacing the current and active SORLCUR record. The replaced student academic program is set to inactive (SORLCUR_CACT_CODE = INACTIVE) and not current (SORLCUR_CURRENT_CDE is null). The field of study records (SORLFOS) associated with the program being replaced are also updated to inactive (SORLFOS_CACT_COD) and not current (SORLFOS_CURRENT_CDE set to null).

Note:

- If the program sent in the request is an SMRPRLE program, at least one major discipline should be sent in 'newProgram.disciplines'.
- If the program sent in the request is a SOBCURR program and if the SOBCURR record does not have any major code attached, at least one major discipline info should be sent in 'newProgram.disciplines'.

The API response conforms to the student-academic-programs v17 data model.

POST requests must include a nil GUID value ("00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000") for the root id property.

In addition to the required properties defined in the data model, the following are required for a POST request: academicPeriods.id


Source OpenAPI file: ethosapis/BannerEedmAPIs/student-academic-programs-replacements-1.0.0/student-academic-programs-replacements.yaml

---

### Student Academic Programs Submissions 1.0.0 - Create a new resource in SORLCUR

Creates a LEARNER curriculum record in SORLCUR table with SORLCUR_LMOD_CODE='LEARNER' and creates the associated field of study records in SORLFOS table for an effective term.

Note:

• If the program sent in the request is an SMRPRLE program, at least one major discipline should be sent in 'newProgram.disciplines'.

• If the program sent in the request is a SOBCURR program and if the SOBCURR record does not have any major code attached, at least one major discipline should be sent in 'newProgram.disciplines'.

The API response conforms to the student-academic-programs v17 data model.

POST requests must include a nil GUID value ("00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000") for the root id property.

In addition to the required properties defined in the data model, the following are required for a POST request: academicPeriods.id

---

### Student Charges 16.0.0 - Returns resources from TBRACCD and TBREACD

Returns a paged listing of all the student charges transactions from TBRACCD and tax reporting charges transactions from TBREACD tables excluding the records with term code ‘ARTERM’. The charge trasactions in TBRACCD and TBREACD are identified through the TBBDETC_TYPE_IND of the accounting code associated with the student charge record in TBRACCD_DETAIL_CODE and TBREACD_DETAIL_CODE.  The TBBDETC_TYPE_IND type of 'C' denotes the transaction as a Charge. The student charges API publishes the non student charges along with student related charges if the configuration setting 'STUD-CHARGES-NONPERSONS' is set to 'Y'. 

Note: The API does not allow to create a non student charge record. For non student charges, the student.id will not be published though it is a required property.


Source OpenAPI file: ethosapis/BannerEedmAPIs/student-charges-16.0.0/student-charges.yaml

parameters.description:
### Student  
The person (student) who incurred the charge.
```

```
### Funding Source  
The accounting code of the funding source associated with the student charge.
```
/student-charges?filter={"fundingSource:": {"id": $id}}
```
### Funding Destination  
The accounting code of the funding destination associated with the student charge.
```
/student-charges?filter={"fundingDestination:": {"id": $id}}
```
### Academic Period  
The term in which charges were incurred.
```
/student-charges?filter={"academicPeriod:": {"id": $id}}
```
### Charge Type  
The type of the charge the student incurred.
```
/student-charges?filter={"chargeType": $chargeType}
```

post.summary: Student Charges 11.0 - Create a new resource
post.description:
### Student Charges 11.0 - Create a new resource

Create a new resource

Source OpenAPI file: ethosapis/BannerEedmAPIs/student-charges-11.0.0/student-charges.yaml

---

### Student Charges 16.0.0 - Create a new resource in TBRACCD and TBREACD

POST requests must include a nil GUID value ("00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000") for the root id property.

Creates a new student charge record. A create request sent with 'reportingDetail' property will create a tax reporting charge transaction record in TBREACD table. A create request sent without 'reportingDetail' property will create a student charge record in TBRACCD table.

For a student charge transaction create request, the API configuration setting 'STUDENT.CHARGES.SOURCE.CODE' can be used to map the appropriate source code from TTVSRCE with TTVSRCE_INPUT_IND = 'Y' to populate the source code value for the transaction in TBRACCD_SRCE_CODE.

The properties 'chargedAmount.unitCost','reportingDetail.activityDates.startOn' and 'reportingDetail.activityDates.endOn' are not supported.

Below are the API behavior details for fundingSource and chargeableOn properties on a create request:

fundingSource:

- The accounting code GUID sent as fundingSource.id should match the fundingDestination.id
- If the fundingDestination.id is not equal to fundingSource.id, the create request will be rejected with an error message

chargeableOn:

- A create request sent for a student charge transaction without reportingDetail property will not allow backdating chargeableOn. Future dates are permitted.
- A create request sent for a tax reporting charge transaction with reportingDetail property will allow backdating chargeableOn.


Source OpenAPI file: ethosapis/BannerEedmAPIs/student-charges-16.0.0/student-charges.yaml

---

Source OpenAPI file: ethosapis/BannerEedmAPIs/student-charges-6.0.0/student-charges.yaml

get.summary: Student Cohort Assignments 1.0.0 - Returns resources from SGRCHRT
get.description:
### Student Cohort Assignments 1.0.0 - Returns resources from SGRCHRT

Returns all cohorts records from SGRCHRT table of all students for the effective term.


Source OpenAPI file: ethosapis/BannerEedmAPIs/student-cohort-assignments-1.0.0/student-cohort-assignments.yaml

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of resources requesting for this result set. Default page limit is 500 and upper limit is 1000.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: The 0 based index for a collection of resources for the page requested.

parameters.name: criteria
parameters.description:
### Person  
The person who has been assigned to a cohort.
```
/student-cohort-assignments?criteria={"person":{"id":"5674f28b-b216-4055-b236-81a922d93b4c"}}
```
### Cohort  
The grouping of students for reporting/tracking purposes (cohorts) to which the student is assigned for a period of time.
```
/student-cohort-assignments?criteria={"cohort": {"id":"42cd0517-fbf8-4340-b852-1becac04792d"}}
```
### Start On  
Supported operators are $gte or the default of equals if operator is not specified.

Returns all student cohort assignments for the start on date passed.
```
/student-cohort-assignments?criteria={"startOn":"1995-02-06"}
```
```
/student-cohort-assignments?criteria={"startOn":{"$eq":"1995-01-01"}}
```
Returns all student cohort assignments with start date greater than or equals to the start on date passed.
```
/student-cohort-assignments?criteria={"startOn":{"$gte":"1995-01-01"}}
```
### End On  
Supported operators are $lte or the default of equals if operator is not specified.

Returns all student cohort assignments for the end on date passed.
```
/student-cohort-assignments?criteria={"endOn":"2016-02-06"}
```
```
/student-cohort-assignments?criteria={"endOn":{"$eq":"2016-01-01"}}
```
Returns all student cohort assignments with end date less than or equals to the end on date passed.
```
/student-cohort-assignments?criteria={"endOn":{"$lte":"2016-01-01"}}
```

get.summary: Student Financial Aid Need Summaries 9.0 - Returns resources from RORSTAT
get.description:
### Student Financial Aid Need Summaries 9.0 - Returns resources from RORSTAT

Returns a paged listing of all the institutional and federal financial aid need summary records from RORSTAT table for the corresponding financial aid application record in RCRAPP1 table.

The institutional applications are the RCRAPP1 records with Information Source Code (RCRAPP1_INFC_CODE ) as CSS or MANUAL and RCRAPP1_SEQ_NO = 0.

The federal applications are the RCRAPP1 records with RCRAPP1_INFC_CODE as EDE or MANUAL, RCRAPP1_SEQ_NO > 0 and RCRAPP1_CURR_REC_IND = 'Y'. The current record of EDE application is published.

The API does not retrieve the RCRAPP1 records with RCRAPP1_INFC_CODE = MANUAL and RCRAPP1_SEQ_NO = -1. These are the records which hold noncustodial parent information and are considered an extension of the RCRAPP1_SEQ_NO = 0 record. The API does not support RCRAPP1_INFC_CODE = 'CAL' records.


Source OpenAPI file: ethosapis/BannerEedmAPIs/student-financial-aid-need-summaries-9.0.0/student-financial-aid-need-summaries.yaml

get.description:
### Student Payments 11.0 - Return all resources

Returns a paged listing of all the resources in the system.

Source OpenAPI file: ethosapis/BannerEedmAPIs/student-payments-11.0.0/student-payments.yaml

---

### Student Payments 16.0.0 - Returns resources from TBRACCD and TBREACD

Returns a paged listing of all the student payment transactions from TBRACCD and tax reporting payment transactions from TBREACD tables excluding the records with term code ‘ARTERM’.
The payment trasactions in TBRACCD and TBREACD are identified through the TBBDETC_TYPE_IND of the accounting code associated with the student payment record in TBRACCD_DETAIL_CODE and TBREACD_DETAIL_CODE.  The TBBDETC_TYPE_IND type of 'P' denotes the transaction as a Payment. The student payments API publishes the non student payments along with student related payments if the configuration setting 'STUD-PAYMENTS-NONPERSONS' is set to 'Y'. 

Note: The API does not allow to create a non student payments record. For non student payments, the student.id will not be published though it is a required property.


Source OpenAPI file: ethosapis/BannerEedmAPIs/student-payments-16.0.0/student-payments.yaml

---

### Student Payments 16.1.0 - Returns resources from TBRACCD and TBREACD

Returns a paged listing of all the student payment transactions from TBRACCD and tax reporting payment transactions from TBREACD tables excluding the records with term code ‘ARTERM’.
The payment trasactions in TBRACCD and TBREACD are identified through the TBBDETC_TYPE_IND of the accounting code associated with the student payment record in TBRACCD_DETAIL_CODE and TBREACD_DETAIL_CODE.  The TBBDETC_TYPE_IND type of 'P' denotes the transaction as a Payment. The student payments API publishes the non student payments along with student related payments if the configuration setting 'STUD-PAYMENTS-NONPERSONS' is set to 'Y'. 

Note: The API does not allow to create a non student payments record. For non student payments, the student.id will not be published though it is a required property.


Source OpenAPI file: ethosapis/BannerEedmAPIs/student-payments-16.1.0/student-payments.yaml

---

### Student Payments 16.0.0 - Create a new resource in TBRACCD and TBREACD

POST requests must include a nil GUID value ("00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000") for the root id property.

Creates a new student payment record. A create request sent with 'reportingDetail' property will create a tax reporting payment transaction record in TBREACD table. A create request sent without 'reportingDetail' property will create a student payment record in TBRACCD table.

Below are the API behavior details for fundingSource and paidOn properties on a create request:

fundingSource:

- The accounting code GUID sent as fundingSource.id should match the fundingDestination.id.
- If the fundingDestination.id is not equal to fundingSource.id, the create request will be rejected with an error message.

paidOn:

- A create request sent for a student payment transaction without reportingDetail property will not allow future dated values.
- A create request sent for a tax reporting payment transaction with reportingDetail property will allow backdating chargeableOn. Request will be rejected if future dated value is sent.


Source OpenAPI file: ethosapis/BannerEedmAPIs/student-payments-16.0.0/student-payments.yaml

### Vendors Maximum 1.0.0 - Returns resources from FTVVEND

Returns a paged list of records from FTVVEND in the Banner system.


Source OpenAPI file: ethosapis/BannerEedmAPIs/vendors-maximum-1.0.0/vendors-maximum.yaml

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of resources requesting for this result set. Default page limit is 500 and upper limit is 1000.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: The 0 based index for a collection of resources for the page requested.

parameters.name: criteria
parameters.description:
### Organization  
Detail information of the organization acting as the vendor.
 ```
/vendors?criteria={"vendorDetail":"0d316912-b8d1-4cbf-ab62-e7bf1f2be576"}
```
```
/vendors?criteria={"vendorDetail":{"organization":{"id":"0d316912-b8d1-4cbf-ab62-e7bf1f2be576"}}}
```
### Person  
Detail information of the person acting as the vendor.
```
/vendors?criteria={"vendorDetail":{"person":{"id":"0d316912-b8d1-4cbf-ab62-e7bf1f2be576"}}}
```
### Institution  
Not Supported

### Classifications  
The vendor's classification (E.g. federal contract, small business, etc. )
```
/vendors?criteria={"classifications":"a174ecf5-15c8-4532-b60c-225f33616de4"}
```
```
/vendors?criteria={"classifications": [{"id":"a174ecf5-15c8-4532-b60c-225f33616de4"}]}
```
### Statuses  
The statuses (active, approved, stop payment) that apply to the vendor.
```
/vendors?criteria={"statuses":["active","approved"]}
```
### Related Vendors Type  
The type of related vendor.
```
/vendors?criteria={"relatedVendors":[{"type":"paymentVendor"}]}
```
### Related Reference Type  
The type of related reference.
```
/vendors?criteria={"relatedReference":[{"type":"parentVendor"}]}
```
### Types  
The type(s) of vendor, i.e. Procurement, eProcurement, or Travel.
```
/vendors?criteria={"types":["procurement","eProcurement"]}
```
### Tax ID  
The federal tax identification number for the vendor.
```
/vendors?criteria={"taxId":"9900"}
```

parameters.name: vendorType
parameters.description:
### Vendor Type  
Returns all vendors for the given vendorType
```
/vendors?vendorType={"vendorType":"procurement"}
```

parameters.name: vendorDetail
parameters.description:
### Vendor Detail  
Filter based on the person, organization or institution acting as the vendor.
```
/vendors?vendorDetail={"vendorDetail": {"id":"1d6dff87-982d-4291-a94b-0f76e7c4277c"}}
```

parameters.name: Accept
parameters.description: Version/media type accepted by this Banner/Ethos endpoint.
